entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The cultivated peanut (Arachis hypogaea L.) characterized by a polyploid genome of hybrid origin.

entities.name: POLYPLOID GENOME
entities.description: A genome containing essentially complete sets of chromosomes from two ancestral species.

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of the peanut genome.

entities.name: MOBILE-ELEMENT ACTIVITY
entities.description: A mechanism of genome evolution involving the movement of genetic elements.

entities.name: DELETIONS
entities.description: A mechanism of genome evolution involving the loss of genetic material.

entities.name: HOMEOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION
entities.description: The flow of genetic information between corresponding ancestral chromosomes.

entities.name: A. MONTICOLA
entities.description: The wild counterpart of the cultivated peanut.

entities.name: POLYPLOID HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids made from ancestral species used to study phenotypic changes.

entities.name: FLOWER COLOR
entities.description: A phenotypic characteristic that can undergo spontaneous changes due to genetic mechanisms.

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild species for human cultivation.

entities.name: DIPLOID ARACHIS SPECIES
entities.description: Non-polyploid species of the Arachis genus cultivated by humans.
